response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned generalist with genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and real low-latency systems chops from Sociomantic, where he built async, high-throughput services in DLang. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his C++ exposure is brief and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project); his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He has no direct routing-tile, map-data pipeline, or navigation-guidance experience, so he would need significant ramp-up on both the core language and the domain-specific pipeline work.
strengths: Authentic geospatial background: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, with GIS work dating to 2013
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, caching, and fibers
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, and a track record of testing tools and community teaching
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, map-tile generation, or navigation guidance systems
Recent work centers on web/product engineering in TypeScript, a substantial shift from systems-level pipeline engineering
